description: 65M post-MI with CKD 3b (eGFR 35). ACC/AHA secondary prevention and KDIGO statin-for-CKD both independently recommend statin therapy — convergence on statin medications via shared clinical entities. Both guidelines recommend moderate-intensity given CKD context. Multiple KDIGO Recs also fire for CKD management.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A: high-intensity statin for clinical ASCVD (post-MI). KDIGO 1A also recommends moderate-intensity statin in CKD G3b (eGFR 35). Both guidelines converge on statin therapy via shared medication entities; CKD context favors moderate intensity. Consider atorvastatin 20mg or rosuvastatin 10mg.

actions.label: Initiate SGLT2 inhibitor for CKD with albuminuria
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1A: SGLT2 inhibitor recommended for CKD with eGFR ≥20 and albuminuria (ACR 180 mg/g, A2 category). Empagliflozin or dapagliflozin. Provides cardiorenal protection in setting of ASCVD + CKD.

actions.label: Initiate ACEi or ARB for albuminuric CKD
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: ACEi or ARB recommended for CKD with albuminuria (ACR ≥30 mg/g). Patient ACR 180 mg/g qualifies. First-line renoprotective therapy.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: regular monitoring of eGFR and urine ACR for CKD staging and progression tracking. CKD G3b with A2 albuminuria indicates high risk.
